_meta.description: WIDA-aligned proficiency adaptations for bilingual teaching strategies

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture dictionaries with L1-L2 visual pairs, teacher modeling of dictionary use with think-alouds
levels_1_2.assessment: Picture identification, gesture responses to vocabulary

levels_3_4.implementation: Bilingual dictionaries with context practice, student-created vocabulary cards with definitions
levels_3_4.assessment: Vocabulary usage in sentences, definition explanations

levels_5_6.implementation: Strategic dictionary use for academic precision, etymology exploration, register awareness

grade_cluster_considerations.K-1: Focus on high-frequency concrete words with visual support
grade_cluster_considerations.2-3: Academic vocabulary introduction with context clues
grade_cluster_considerations.4-5: Complex academic terms with morphological analysis
grade_cluster_considerations.9-12: Sophisticated academic register and nuanced meanings

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture-heavy word cards with L1-L2 pairs, color coding for categories, interactive pointing games
levels_1_2.assessment: Word recognition, category sorting, basic usage

levels_3_4.implementation: Interactive word wall games, student-created cards, morphology highlighting, sentence frame integration

levels_5_6.implementation: Student-led vocabulary instruction, etymology displays, academic register distinctions, cross-curricular connections
levels_5_6.assessment: Vocabulary instruction leadership, complex usage demonstrations

grade_cluster_considerations.K-1: Basic nouns and verbs with pictures, alphabet organization

levels_1_2.implementation: 3-5 key words with visual supports, L1 cognate connections, TPR integration, repeated exposure
levels_1_2.assessment: Word recognition, simple matching, gesture responses

levels_3_4.implementation: Academic vocabulary pre-teaching with graphic organizers, word analysis, context practice
levels_3_4.assessment: Definition provision, contextual usage, explanation tasks

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex vocabulary instruction with nuanced meanings, register awareness, etymology connections
levels_5_6.assessment: Sophisticated usage, register appropriateness, complex explanations

levels_1_2.implementation: Visual cognate matching, side-by-side L1-L2 displays, pronunciation practice

levels_3_4.implementation: Cognate hunting activities, false cognate awareness, morphological pattern recognition
levels_3_4.assessment: Cognate discovery, accurate usage, pattern application

levels_5_6.implementation: Etymology analysis, register variations of cognates, academic cognate networks
levels_5_6.assessment: Etymology explanations, register distinctions, network creation

levels_1_2.implementation: Simple templates with visual cues, L1 labeling allowed, teacher modeling with think-alouds

levels_3_4.implementation: Guided completion with sentence frames, bilingual vocabulary support, peer collaboration
levels_3_4.assessment: Organized thinking, sentence frame usage, collaborative participation

levels_5_6.implementation: Independent creation of complex organizers, academic language integration, critical analysis support
levels_5_6.assessment: Independent organizer creation, complex analysis, synthesis demonstration

levels_1_2.implementation: Picture-rich texts, frequent pausing for comprehension, L1 discussion time, gesture responses
levels_1_2.assessment: Gesture responses, picture identification, L1 comprehension checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Strategic stopping points, sentence frames for responses, partner discussions, vocabulary pre-teaching
levels_3_4.assessment: Prediction accuracy, summary completion, question formation

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex text analysis, academic discourse modeling, critical thinking questions, independent responses
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex analysis, academic discourse, independent critical responses

levels_1_2.implementation: Heavy L1 support for comprehension, visual bridging to English, peer translation assistance
levels_1_2.assessment: Comprehension demonstration, basic English output with L1 support

levels_3_4.implementation: Strategic L1 use for complex concepts, bilingual brainstorming, structured bridging activities
levels_3_4.assessment: Complex idea processing, strategic language use, bridging demonstration

levels_5_6.implementation: Sophisticated translanguaging for academic purposes, metalinguistic awareness development, register flexibility
levels_5_6.assessment: Strategic language use, metalinguistic analysis, register adaptation

levels_1_2.implementation: Heavy visual and hands-on supports, simplified language objectives, frequent comprehension checks

levels_3_4.implementation: Structured interaction opportunities, academic language scaffolds, graphic organizer support

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex academic tasks, sophisticated language objectives, independent application
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex task completion, sophisticated language use

levels_1_2.implementation: Structured partner work with visual supports, L1 discussion encouraged, simple role assignments

levels_3_4.implementation: Small group protocols, sentence frames for discussion, rotating leadership roles
levels_3_4.assessment: Protocol following, academic discussion, leadership demonstration

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex collaborative tasks, academic argumentation, peer teaching opportunities
levels_5_6.assessment: Complex collaboration, academic argumentation, peer teaching

levels_1_2.implementation: Non-verbal response options, picture-based assessments, L1 explanation allowed
levels_1_2.assessment: Non-verbal demonstration, picture identification, L1 explanation

levels_3_4.implementation: Multiple assessment modalities, sentence frame supports, peer assessment opportunities
levels_3_4.assessment: Multi-modal demonstration, frame usage, peer assessment

levels_5_6.implementation: Self-assessment tools, academic language focus, complex reasoning demonstration
levels_5_6.assessment: Self-assessment accuracy, academic language use, complex reasoning

levels_1_2.implementation: Cultural artifact sharing, family photo discussions, simple cultural comparisons with L1 support
levels_1_2.assessment: Cultural sharing, family descriptions, basic comparisons

levels_3_4.implementation: Structured cultural presentations, heritage language connections, community knowledge integration
levels_3_4.assessment: Cultural presentations, heritage connections, knowledge sharing

levels_5_6.implementation: Complex cultural analysis, critical cultural discussions, academic cultural research
levels_5_6.assessment: Cultural analysis, critical discussions, academic research

levels_1_2.implementation: Family skill sharing with visual supports, simple community connections, L1 storytelling
